title: Lindsay Clancy back in court for final hearing before murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lindsay Clancy lost a bid to include testimony from other women with postpartum mental illness for her murder trial.
Jury selection for Clancy's trial, involving three counts of first-degree murder, will begin on July 20, in Plymouth.
Judge Sullivan rejected layperson testimony; expert witnesses will provide information on postpartum mental health issues.

title: ESPN ranks Drake Maye among NFL’s top-10 quarterbacks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Drake Maye ranked No. 8 on ESPN's NFL top-10 quarterbacks list after being unranked last season.
Maye led the NFL in quarterback rating, yards per completion, and completion percentage, while also rushing for 450 yards.
Maye's performance elevated the Patriots to a 14-3 record and a Super Bowl appearance, despite lacking star receivers.

title: Australian wins first-ever Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Australian James Webb won the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship by eating 7.77 pounds of wings in 10 minutes.
Webb defeated 18-time Nathan’s Famous Hot Dog champion Joey Chestnut, who ate 7.54 pounds, and Massachusetts native Geoff Esper.
The championship held at The Brook in Seabrook, N.H. awarded Webb $2,500 for his victory in the competition.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Linebackers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots' linebackers performed at a high level under coach Mike Vrabel last season, ranking third by Pro Football Focus.
The team faces challenges with Harold Landry's knee injury and the departure of key players but maintains a strong core.
Rookie Gabe Jacas remains unsigned, while Elijah Ponder shows promising development in offseason training sessions.

title: Man dead after apparent drowning in Randolph
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A man died after an apparent drowning in Great Pond in Randolph on Sunday afternoon.
Emergency services responded to a 911 call about a swimmer in distress at around 4 p.m. at Pond Street.
State Police are investigating the incident; the man's identity has not been disclosed.

title: Historic Somerville Theatre and Capitol Theatre buildings hit the market
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Somerville Theatre and Capitol Theatre buildings are listed for sale at $12 million each.
The properties include a 900-seat theater and 500-capacity ballroom in Davis Square, and 18 apartments in Arlington.
CSB Theatres holds multi-year leases on both sites, ensuring business stability despite building sale uncertainties.

title: Matt Turner’s loan with the Revolution extended; New England has option to buy GK from Lyon
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution extended Matt Turner's loan from Lyon through December 31, 2026, with an option to buy his contract.
Turner, no longer a Designated Player, is now a TAM-eligible player, impacting the club's salary cap flexibility.
Turner's retention strengthens the Revolution's squad as they prepare for the second half of the MLS season.

title: Trooper injured in wrong-way crash on Mass. Pike in Chicopee
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A wrong-way car accident on I-91 in Chicopee injured a Massachusetts State Police trooper, his K-9, and another driver.
Jose G Santiago Jr. faces charges including DUI and negligent operation after a breathalyzer showed 0.19% alcohol level.
All injured parties were taken to a hospital and are expected to recover, according to State Police Colonel Geoffrey Noble.

title: Here’s how hot and humid it could get this week, according to Boston meteorologists
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A Heat Advisory is in effect from 11 AM Tuesday to 8 PM Wednesday for much of southern New England.
Temperatures in Boston are projected to reach the mid-90s, feeling like 95-103°F with high humidity.
A risk of severe thunderstorms is expected on Tuesday in northern New England, especially near the Canadian border.

title: Red Sox used a 9th round pick in MLB Draft on an intriguing power-hitting prospect
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Red Sox drafted 18-year-old power-hitter Martin Shelar from Georgia in the 9th round of the MLB Draft.
Shelar hit over .500 with 19 home runs in his senior year at Marist School and recorded a 115.5 mph exit velocity.
Scouts praise his power but question his contact consistency; he's committed to Mississippi State but may go pro.
